title: High voltage switch
abstract: Disclosed is circuitry for operating a switch which sees high voltage swings across its source, gate, drain, and bulk terminals. The circuitry generates one or more bias voltages in proportion to an input voltage swing.. The one or more bias voltages may be used to bias the gate and bulk terminals to provide reliable and improved turn OFF performance in the switch.
claims: Claims 1. A circuit comprising: first and second input terminals for a connection to a source of first and second input voltages, wherein the first and second input voltages are differential in nature; an output circuit having output terminals for a connection to a load, the output circuit comprising at least one transistor device, the at least one transistor device having a gate terminal and a bulk terminal; and a sampling circuit having first and second inputs in electrical connection with the first and second input terminals, the sampling circuit further having an output connected to the gate terminal and the bulk terminal of the at least one transistor device, the sampling circuit operative to produce a voltage level at the output thereof responsive to voltage levels at the first and second input terminals. 2. The circuit of claim 1 wherein the sampling circuit is further operative to produce a voltage level at the output thereof that is proportional to a voltage swing between the first and second input terminals. 3. The circuit of claim 1 wherein the sampling circuit comprises a rectifier circuit. 4. The circuit of claim 1 wherein the sampling circuit comprises a peak detector. 5. The circuit of claim 1 wherein the sampling circuit comprises a self-mixing local oscillator. 6. The circuit of claim 1 wherein the output circuit further comprises a second transistor device having a gate terminal and a bulk terminal, wherein the output of the sampling circuit is further connected to the gate terminal and the bulk terminal of the second transistor device. 7. The circuit of claim 1 wherein the at least one transistor device is a field effect transistor ( FET). 8. A circuit comprising: first and second input terminals; first and second output terminals; a network connected between the input terminals and the output terminals; a switching circuit having a source terminal, a drain terminal, at least one gate terminal, and at least one bulk terminal, the input terminals connected to the source and drain terminals; a sampling circuit having an input in electrical connection with the input terminals, the sampling circuit having at least one output, the at least one output of the sampling circuit connected to the at least one gate terminal and the at least one bulk terminal of the switching circuit, the sampling circuit operative to generate a voltage level at the least one output thereof based on input signals at the input terminals. 9. The circuit of claim 8 wherein the sampling circuit is operative to generate a voltage level that is proportional to a voltage swing between the first input terminal and the second input terminal. 10. The circuit of claim 8 wherein the sampling circuit is connected to sampling points in the network. 11. The circuit of claim 10 wherein the network comprises a first inductor and a second inductor. 12. The circuit of claim 8 wherein the switching circuit comprises a first transistor device and a second transistor device, a source of the first transistor device connected to the drain of the second transistor device. 13. The circuit of claim 12 wherein the sampling circuit is connected to gate terminals and bulk terminals of the first and second transistor devices. 14. The circuit of claim 8 wherein the switching circuit comprises a single transistor device. 15. A circuit comprising: first and second input terminals; a switching circuit having a source terminal, a drain terminal, at least one gate terminal, and at least one bulk terminal, the input terminals connected to the source and drain terminals; and means for generating a voltage level responsive to a voltage swing at the first and second input terminals, the means for generating having at least one output connected to the at least one gate terminal and the at least one bulk terminal, the means for generating operative to bias the at least one gate terminal and the at least one bulk terminal at voltage levels higher than voltage levels at the source terminal and the drain terminal. 16. The circuit of claim 15 wherein the means for generating comprises a rectifier circuit. 17. The circuit of claim 15 wherein the means for generating comprises a peak detector. 18. The circuit of claim 15 wherein the means for generating comprises a self-mixing local oscillator. 19. The circuit of claim 15 wherein the switching circuit comprises a first transistor device and a second transistor device, a source of the first transistor device connected to the drain of the second transistor device. 20. The circuit of claim 19 wherein a drain of the first transistor device is connected to the drain terminal of the switching circuit and a source of the second transistor device is connected to the source terminal of the switching circuit.
